name: AIDS Origin Theories
description: AIDS origin theories explore the disputed origins of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV), which causes Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome (AIDS). While the mainstream scientific consensus, supported by extensive genetic and epidemiological evidence, points to a zoonotic transfer of Simian Immunodeficiency Virus (SIV) from chimpanzees to humans in Central Africa in the early 20th century, alternative theories propose different origins, often involving human intervention or experimentation. These theories range from accidental contamination of polio vaccines to deliberate creation and release of the virus as a bioweapon. Proponents of these alternative theories often cite inconsistencies in the official narrative, historical events like unethical medical experiments, and perceived biases in scientific research. Mainstream scientists and public health officials strongly refute these alternative theories, citing the overwhelming evidence supporting the natural zoonotic origin and the potential harm caused by spreading misinformation that undermines trust in medical science and public health efforts to combat the AIDS epidemic. Criticisms of alternative theories often focus on flawed interpretations of scientific data, reliance on anecdotal evidence, and the lack of credible scientific support.
subtitle: Disputed origins of the AIDS epidemic.

claims.evidence: Allegations that early polio vaccines were grown in chimpanzee kidney cells infected with SIV, which then mutated into HIV in humans.
claims.criticism: Extensive testing of archived polio vaccines has found no evidence of HIV or SIV. The polio vaccine theory also fails to account for the genetic diversity of HIV strains.

claims.claim: AIDS was deliberately created as a bioweapon.
claims.evidence: Claims of secret government programs and alleged statements by scientists suggesting the possibility of engineering viruses. Also, the disproportionate impact on certain communities.
claims.criticism: There is no credible scientific evidence to support the claim of deliberate creation. The complexity of HIV's genetic structure and evolution makes it highly improbable it was engineered. The claim also relies on circumstantial evidence and misinterpretations.

claims.claim: HIV existed prior to the 20th century but was misdiagnosed.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts of illnesses resembling AIDS symptoms from before the officially recognized start of the epidemic.
claims.criticism: These accounts lack definitive medical confirmation. The absence of documented HIV infections or AIDS cases before the mid-20th century, combined with genetic evidence, contradicts this claim.

keyFigures.name: Robert Gallo
keyFigures.role: Co-discoverer of HIV and initially implicated in vaccine contamination theories (later exonerated).

keyFigures.name: Peter Duesberg
keyFigures.role: Prominent AIDS denialist who disputes the link between HIV and AIDS.

keyFigures.name: David Ho
keyFigures.role: Pioneering researcher in AIDS treatment who demonstrated HIV's causal role in AIDS.

mythologyConnections: Scapegoating (blaming specific groups for the disease)

timeline.event: Zoonotic transfer of SIV to humans in Central Africa (mainstream theory).
Development and widespread use of polio vaccines.
First official reports of AIDS cases in the United States.
Development and spread of various AIDS origin theories.
